title: rbac — subprocessors
description: RBAC does not engage third-party subprocessors. Every read + write is against the platform's own Postgres + Redis. This file documents the empty surface so compliance reviewers can quickly verify.
notes: Data never leaves the platform for RBAC operations. Role + permission strings, role assignments, and cached ResolvedPermissionSets stay on the platform's owned infrastructure.
The permission catalogue itself (permission strings + descriptions) is CODE-shipped — the source is in git, seeded via PermissionCatalogueLoader. No external catalogue provider is engaged.
The `symfony/expression-language` grammar used by workflow/approvals (Wave 1b sibling) is a vendored open-source library, NOT a subprocessor — no data is sent to Symfony's servers.

Downstream notifications (RoleAssignedNotification, etc.) are dispatched via the notifications module, which engages email/SMS subprocessors documented in notifications/subprocessors.json. RBAC itself does not engage them directly.
